calendar.matters:
Petition To Decrease Bond
Off Calendar Oct-24-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Katelyn Wheeler appeared by zoom for Konstantin Lopyrev.
Heidi Kaden (surviving spouse) appeared by zoom in propria persona.

Mrs. Kaden confirmed that she has received the stocks as well as the cash that was held in the Mindkick account. Mrs. Wheeler stated that all of the assets have been distributed out of the estate at this point, that there was a discrepancy of $40.00, that she will be closing this out soon and that they will do an informal accounting of distribution of the $50,000.00 reserve. The court explained the purpose and the reason for reducing the bond. Mrs. Kaden stated that she does not object to the petition. The Petition to Decrease Bond is under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Bastain. Not reported.

Petition To Decrease Bond
Off Calendar Oct-02-2023 Continued To Oct-23-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-12-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Katelyn Wheeler, via Zoom, for and with Konstantin Lopyrev, Administrator; Heidi Kaden, via Zoom, in propria persona, decedent's surviving spouse.

The court advises that it has reviewed the amended petition and that it appears to the court that the only issue to discuss today is regarding the distribution of the burden of extraordinary fees. Ms. Kaden offers advocacy on behalf of her position that she should not be liable for 100% of the extraordinary fees. Ms. Wheeler and Mr. Lopyrev offer testimony as to why Ms. Kaden should be responsible for 100% of extraordinary fees, as prayed for in the amended petition, specifically, but not limited to, costs incurred by the estate for her failure to vacate the estate's real property. The court acknowledges the position of each side regarding the preceding issue and offers its position. The court advises the parties of its tentative ruling to reduce Ms. Kaden's distribution by 25%.

After additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-25-2022 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Mar-09-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Heidi Kaden, via ZOOM, in propria persona, Petitioner; Katelyn Wheeler, via ZOOM, for Konstantin Lopyrev, via ZOOM, Administrator.

Ms. Kaden advises that she disagrees with the Administrator's objection to her petition and offers oral argument to support her petition specifically regarding Tesla stocks and using estate funds to hire a forensic accountant. Ms. Wheeler and Mr. Lopyrev offer advocacy for their positions and objections to Ms. Kaden's petition noting that the hiring of the forensic accountant was required to determine whether Tesla and Google stocks are community property and that it would be unfair to the remaining heirs to bear the full cost of administration considering Ms. Kaden's actions regarding this matter, including not vacating the estate's real property and delaying the resolution of this matter by filing numerous objections. The court advises the parties that it is inclined to take this petition under submission with the intent to deny it without prejudice.

The Petition for Spousal Property is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Gomez. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Final Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Jul-21-2021 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Order Of Occupancy Rights
Off Calendar Jul-21-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Katelyn Wheeler, via ZOOM, for Konstantin Lopyrev, via ZOOM, Petitioner; Sara Summerall, via ZOOM, for Heidi Kaden, via ZOOM, Objector.

At the outset, Ms. Summerall reports that she is appearing to advise the court that her client has decided to move forward in propria persona. The court confirms the preceding with Ms. Kaden and excuses Me. Summerall. The court then confirms with Ms. Kaden that she is currently residing at the Folsom St. property and reports that she intends to vacate the property at the end of July or by August. Ms. Wheeler states that she would like for Ms. Kaden to vacate the property sooner so that preparations can begin to clean and list the property for sale. The court then reminds Ms. Kaden of the court's previous order which stated that she had no occupancy rights. The court then advises her that she may be liable for any expenses that the estate incurs by her continuing to reside at the property. Ms. Kaden and Ms. Wheeler offer additional testimony. Ms. Kaden then agrees in open court that she will vacate the property by August 15, 2021. Ms. Wheeler then requests a bond increase to a total liability of $2,465,000, without a petition. The court grants the request and advises counsel to contact the assigned Examiner regarding forwarding a proposed order thereon. The court then confirms with Ms. Wheeler that the Status on Order of Occupancy Rights can be taken off calendar by the court.

The Status on Order of Occupancy Rights is taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Final Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Jun-23-2021 Continued To Jul-21-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Katelyn Wheeler, via ZOOM, for Konstantin Lopyrev, via ZOOM, Administrator; Heidi Kaden, via ZOOM, surviving spouse.

The court begins by asking Ms. Wheeler regarding the status of the Final Inventory and Appraisal (I&A). Ms. Wheeler reports that a partial I&A has been filed and that she is working with the probate referee to complete the Final I&A. The court and Ms. Wheeler then turn to the question of the bond amount. Ms. Wheeler reports that an ex parte application to increase bond has been submitted to the court and requests the court increase the bond amount without the need for the court to review the ex parte application and issue a subsequent order. The court confirms with Ms. Wheeler that the current bond amount is $1,7000,000 and that she is requesting an increase of $795,000 for a total bond liability of $2,495,000. The court grants Ms. Wheeler's request and orders the bond increased to the previously mentioned total liability amount, in open court. The court then confirms with Ms. Kaden that she is still represented by Ms. Summerall. The court, counsel, Ms. Kaden and Mr. Lopyrev then discuss the issues with the real property currently occupied by Ms. Kaden, specifically the court order dated March 29, 2021. Mr. Lopyrev reports that Ms. Kaden has not moved from the property, and that it has been difficult for him to access the property to retrieve items that belong to his brother's estate. Ms. Kaden reports that she will be working with Mr. Lopyrev to arrange for him to visit the real property.

After additional discussion, the Status on Filing a Final Inventory and Appraisal is continued to July 21, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204. A Status on Order of Occupancy Rights is set for the same date, time, and location. An Order to Appear will be issued to Attorney Sarah Summerall, to appear on July 21, 2021, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Final Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar May-26-2021 Continued To Jun-23-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Katelyn Wheeler, via ZOOM, counsel for Konstantin Lopyrev, via ZOOM, Administrator; Heidi Kaden, via ZOOM, surviving spouse.

The court begins by asking Ms. Wheeler how much more time she needs to file a Final Inventory and Appraisal. Ms. Wheeler reports that the partial Inventory and Appraisal filed did not include some additional assets which have yet to be appraised. She reports that she intends to file a Final Inventory and Appraisal as soon as the assets have been evaluated. She also reports that an ex parte order to increase bond may be filed, depending on the appraisal of the additional assets.

After brief discussion, the Status of Filing of Inventory and Appraisal is continued to June 23, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Final Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Apr-26-2021 Continued To May-26-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Mar-22-2021 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Setting Aside Probate Homestead; Petition For Family Allowance
Off Calendar Mar-26-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Katelyn Wheeler appeared by zoom for and with Konstantin Lopyrev (Administrator) by zoom.
Alexander Johnson appeared by zoom for and with Heidi Kaden (Petitioner) by zoom.

Mr. Johnson stated that he read the objections by Ms. Wheeler, but he hasn't had a chance to respond, that he received the Probate Examiner's notes regarding the spousal property petition ("SPP"), that he doesn't necessarily disagree with the fact that it will require some complicated tracing and that he would like a chance to speak with Counsel to see if they can agree with regard to characterization of the property. Ms. Wheeler stated that she is willing to sit down with Mr. Johnson to see what they offer. Ms. Wheeler also raised the issue of delay in getting an inventory, stated that the Petitioner is very slow in filing anything and responding, and requested a strict schedule. The Court stated that the current spousal property petition is inadequate and needs to be amended, and informed Mr. Johnson that it is his Client's obligation to characterize the asset. Ms. Wheeler stated that if the SPP is to go forward, it needs to be properly amended and re-served, that she filed an ex parte to hire someone to help with characterization of the property, or the SPP could be dismissed and they can start all over. With respect to the probate homestead and the allowance amounts, Ms. Wheeler stated that her objections are very clear. The Court asked Mr. Johnson to e-mail an order to Ms. Hilton and stated that it will review the ex parte petition. Ms. Wheeler stated that she will assist Mr. Lopyrev regarding his questions about the inventory and that they are having problems with being able to take a look at the personal items. Ms. Kaden stated that she is happy to provide photos. The Spousal Property Petition is off calendar. The Petition for Order Setting Aside Probate Homestead and for Family Allowance is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jan-11-2021 Continued To Mar-22-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Dec-21-2020 Continued To Jan-11-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-02-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Katelyn Wheeler appeared by zoom for and with Alexander Lopyrev by zoom, Valeria Lopryev by zoom and Konstain Lopryev by zoom.
Sarah Summerall appeared by zoom for and with Heidi Kaden by zoom.
Rowena Navia appeared by zoom for John Balquist (Proposed Administrator).

Ms. Wheeler informed the Court that the Parties did not agree to a neutral professional fiduciary. The Court stated that it looked at the case very closely and that the state of affairs today is that Ms. Kaden does not have priority because of the filing of the dissolution prior to the Decedent's death. The Court also spoke of the issue that was raised by Ms. Kaden regarding the actions taken by Konstantin prior to appointment being grounds for removal, stated that it would have to find causes for removal under Probate Code 8502 and that it does not see anything in the documents filed by Ms. Kaden that rises to the level of removal. Ms. Summerall stated that Konstantin does not respect Heidi's status and that he has committed fraud as far as contacting other agencies indicating that Heidi and the Decedent were not married. The Court indicated that it does not see what was stated by Konstantin as a direct statement. Ms. Kaden stated that Konstantin continues to indicate that she is not married to the Decedent, that this is not a good basis to start a probate proceeding, that Konstantin is not a trust worthy individual, that he is going behind her back and that he is not informing her of anything. Ms. Wheeler stated that there is no evidence or background to support the allegations, that nobody has authority to proceed with the estate and that Konstantin can't provide any information. Ms. Summerall stated that Konstantin took some documents and never returned them to Heidi, and that Heidi needs them for personal reasons. Konstantin stated that he has been trying to have a relationship with Heidi and that he has given Heidi's Lawyer all of the documents he had. The Court stated that the actions taken by Konstantin does not rise to the level of removal and that Konstantin remains in the position of priority. The Petition for Letters of Administration filed by Heidi Kaden is denied. The court is granting the Petition for Letters of Administration filed by Konstantin Lopyrev. Bond is set at $1,700,000.00. Status on filing of a final inventory and appraisal is set for April 26, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on close of estate is set for April 25, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Konstantin is under submission for review of the order.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-26-2020 Denied

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-21-2020 Continued To Oct-26-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-21-2020 Continued To Oct-26-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance: Katelyn Wheeler appeared by zoom for and with Konstantin Lopyrev by zoom and for Alexander Lopyrev and Valeria Lopyrev.
Sara Summerall appeared by zoom for and with Heidi Kaden (Petitioner) by zoom.
Rowena Navia appeared by zoom for and with John Balquist (Proposed Professional Fiduciary) by zoom.

The Court stated that notice to the Canadian Consulate must be given under Probate Code 8113, and asked Ms. Summerall if the issue is about the dissolution petition and how it affects the course of this case under Probate Code 8461, and stated that it appears her asking for a Professional acknowledges that her Client may have lost her status of first in line to serve, but also due to some of the actions taken by the Family. Ms. Wheeler stated that the Family was acting in good faith all along including making sure Heidi had a place to live, and that the Family has a competent Administrator. The Petitions for Letters of Administration (2) are continued to October 26, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for proper notice to the Canadian Consulate.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Court Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-14-2020 Continued To Sep-21-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-10-2020 Continued To Sep-14-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Katelyn Wheeler appearing by Zoom for Konstantin Lopyrev, Alexander Lopyrev, and Valeria Lopyrev. Sara Summerall appearing by CourtCall for Heidi Kaden who was appearing by Zoom.

The Court stated that it had reviewed the file and inquired about Probate Code 8463. Ms. Summerall stated that they were modifying their objection to reflect "unfit to serve" and that there is no dispute that they were legitimately separated at the time. The Court stated that the petition was not framed properly. Ms. Wheeler stated that there is a clear proper procedure as to how to go about this, that it has been eight months since the date of death, that there was plenty of time to file objections, that any further delay will result in harm, that they have contacted the creditors, and that the Court should appoint the person that has priority. The Court stated that Ms. Summerall has raised other issues and that it will allow a brief continuance for her to reframe her petition. Ms. Wheeler objected to any further delay and requested that the continuance be a short set. Ms. Kaden addressed the Court stating that she is the surviving spouse of the decedent, that she has legitimate concerns regarding the petitioner, that there is an issue with trustworthiness and not being forthcoming, that she was told that this matter would be handled in the best interest of the decedent's parents, and that people are being told that she was already divorced. Ms. Kaden further stated that she is not sure that this matter will be managed in the best interest of all of the beneficiaries and that there has already been access to emails, etc. The Court addressed Ms. Summerall stating that she would need to reframe her petition. Ms. Summerall stated that she is working with the other side. Ms. Wheeler stated that the opposition was filed at the last minute and that she wanted sufficient time to respond. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to September 14, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Summerall is to file by August 19, 2020 to allow time for Ms. Wheeler to respond. The Court requested that courtesy copies be delivered to the court. The Court stated that if the parties work it out that they should let the Court know. Ms. Wheeler stated that they had successful discussions and noted that there were delays in getting responses.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Probate Examiner: Probate Director Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.
